_comment: Special-element annotation overlay for Book I Episode 5. Covers all non-standard printed elements per the Hart Concordance Symbols/Math pages. Source: Hart Concordance (rosenlake.net); 1939 Faber & Faber edition.
source: Hart Concordance Symbols page; McHugh (1976) pp. 133-134; 1939 edition text supplied for 121.03, 121.07, 124.09, 124.10.
encoding_note: See data/annotations/README.md for the full sigla Unicode reference table.

Ǝ.note: The text describes it as 'the meant to be baffling chrismon trilithon sign...finally called after some his hes hecitency Hec'. Stripped by Trent, leaving 'sign , finally'.

△.note: The text describes it as 'the smaller...fontly called following a certain change of state of grace of nature alp or delta'. Stripped by Trent, leaving 'smaller , fontly'.

Ⅎ.note: McHugh (1976) p.136: 'laterally inverted pairs of Fs'. No standard Unicode equivalent; U+2132 is the closest. Both the 121.03 and 121.07 variants are represented by the same codepoint — they are distinguishable only by position.

Λ.note: Appears between an arrow (124.08, → U+2192, confirmed in corpus) and an equals sign (124.10, = U+003D, confirmed in corpus). In the 1939 text: 'ay [Λ] fork, of à grave Brofèsor'. Joyce draws on Bertrand Russell's Introduction to Mathematical Philosophy (1919) throughout the math notation in I.5 and II.2.

math.note: CONFIRMED IN CORPUS. → (U+2192 RIGHTWARDS ARROW) survives in I.5 line 124.08. 1939 context: 'pointed out → that they ad bîn "provoked"'.

CONFIRMED IN CORPUS. = (U+003D) survives in I.5 line 124.10. 1939 text: 'to = introduce' — spaces stripped in telelib, producing 'to=introdùce'. The Trent line should preserve or restore the spaces. Confirmed as the concordance 124.10 equals sign, not a hyphenation artifact.

annotations.note: Concordance 119.17. Follows 'chrismon trilithon sign'; space before comma is the stripped siglum gap. 1939 context: 'sign [Ǝ], finally called after some his hes hecitency Hec'. char_offset is -1 pending verification against Trent corpus line 119.17.

Concordance 119.18. Follows 'smaller'; space before comma is the stripped siglum gap. 1939 context: 'as the smaller [△], fontly called following a certain change of state of grace of nature alp or delta'. char_offset is -1 pending verification against Trent corpus line 119.18.

Concordance 121.03: 'upside-down backwards F'. 1939 text: 'page, broods [Ⅎ] sensationseeking an idea, amid the verbiage'. Corpus has 'broods sensationseeking' — siglum stripped, one space survives. char_offset is -1 pending verification against Trent corpus line 121.03.

Concordance 121.07: 'upside-down F'. 1939 text: 'returns inhibited, with some half-halted suggestion, [Ⅎ], dragging'. Corpus has 'suggestion, , dragging' — siglum stripped between two commas; double-comma pattern is the detectable trace. char_offset is -1 pending verification against Trent corpus line 121.07.

Concordance 124.09: 'lambda'. 1939 text: 'ad bin "provoked" ay [Λ] fork, of à grave Brofèsor'. Corpus has 'ay fork' — siglum stripped, space survives. Arrow (124.08) and equals (124.10) confirmed in corpus. char_offset is -1 pending verification against Trent corpus line 124.09.
